tasks.instruction: Create a new chatbot named "Store Support Bot" using the first template in the gallery that is labeled for both customer support and e‑commerce, set its primary language to English, and give it the greeting "Hi! I can help you with orders, shipping, and returns today."

expected_values.greeting_message: Hi! I can help you with orders, shipping, and returns today.

tasks.instruction: Add exactly three FAQ entries to an existing chatbot’s knowledge base: one about shipping time, one about the return policy, and one about checking order status, each assigned to an appropriate category.

faqs.question: How long does shipping take?
faqs.answer: Standard shipping takes 3–5 business days within the US.

faqs.question: What is your return policy?
faqs.answer: You can return unused items within 30 days for a full refund.

faqs.question: How can I check my order status?
faqs.answer: You can track your order using the tracking link sent after purchase or by providing your order ID in the chat.

tasks.instruction: For any existing chatbot, customize the chat widget so its main accent color is set to hex code #1A73E8, it appears in the bottom-right corner, and its header title reads "Need help with your order?", then save the appearance settings.

tasks.instruction: Create a routing rule that sends chats received Monday through Friday between 9:00 AM and 5:00 PM to the team responsible for pre-sales questions and pricing inquiries, keeps all other times handled by the bot only, and enable the rule.

tasks.instruction: Invite two new team members named "Alex Admin" and "Emma Editor" using the email addresses alex.admin@example.com and emma.editor@example.com, assign one the highest-permission role and the other the next lower-permission role, then assign the lower-permission member as a collaborator on any existing chatbot.
